metadata.title: TmpMail - Secure, Instant, Fast Temporary Email
metadata.description: Protect your personal email address from spam, bots, phishing, and other online abuse with TmpMail.

header.mailCheckDisabledDesc: No longer auto-refreshing, click refresh manually
header.mailAutoCheckOffDesc: Won't auto-poll, manually refresh for new emails
header.accountSwitchFailedDesc: Please try logging in to this account again

sidebar.guestModeDesc: Create a disposable inbox in one click and keep the credentials in this browser.

emptyState.title: Temp mail, kept simple
emptyState.domainSetupHint: Add and verify a real domain in the admin console before creating inboxes.
emptyState.flowDescription: A disposable inbox without signup friction.
emptyState.previewHint: No active domain is configured yet.
emptyState.stepCreateDescription: Create a random address and password in one click without exposing your main mailbox.
emptyState.stepReceiveDescription: The inbox updates in real time so verification codes and signup emails land here immediately.
emptyState.stepResetDescription: Use the mailbox for one task, then drop it without carrying long-term spam risk.
emptyState.ctaHint: Use a short-lived mailbox for registration, testing, and one-off downloads. Keep anything important in your permanent inbox.

featureCards.secureDesc: Random passwords stay in this browser.
featureCards.instantDesc: No signup flow. Open and use it.
featureCards.fastDesc: Codes and notifications land in the current inbox quickly.

messageList.emptyDesc: You haven't received any emails yet. When you do, they'll show up here as beautiful cards.

messageList.refreshError: Failed to refresh emails. Please try again.
messageList.errorHelp: The inbox stayed visible. Try another refresh while the live stream reconnects.

messageDetail.fetchError: Failed to fetch email details. Please try again.
messageDetail.deleteError: Failed to delete email. Please try again.

accountModal.title: Create Account
accountModal.autoModeHint: Leave the fields empty to generate a usable inbox and random password automatically.
accountModal.manualModeHint: Fill in username, domain, and password to create an inbox with your own address.
accountModal.usernameTooShort: Username must be at least 3 characters

accountModal.fillAllFields: Please fill in all required fields
accountModal.otpUnavailable: Email OTP is not enabled right now. Please contact the administrator.
accountModal.otpAutoGenerateDisabled: Auto-generated inboxes are unavailable while email OTP is enabled. Enter an address manually and verify it first.

accountModal.otpSentDescription: A verification code was sent to this email address and expires in {seconds} seconds.

accountModal.otpHint: Before creating the inbox, send a one-time verification code to this email address and enter it here.
accountModal.emailTaken: This email address is already in use. If this is your account, you can try logging in.
accountModal.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again.
accountModal.createFailed: Failed to create account, please try again later
accountModal.loginFailed: Login failed. Please check your password or try creating a new account with a different username.

loginModal.splitModeHint: Best when you remember the username and domain but do not want to retype the full address.
loginModal.fullModeHint: Best when you want to paste the full mailbox address in one step.
loginModal.usernameTooShort: Username must be at least 3 characters
loginModal.usernameTooLong: Username must be 64 characters or fewer

settings.apiKeySectionDescription: Generate and view the management API key for the current signed-in account here. The domains page reuses it automatically.
settings.apiKeyGeneratedDescription: A usable management API key was generated or synced for the current account.
settings.apiKeyGenerateFailed: Failed to prepare management API key
settings.apiKeyGenerateFailedDesc: Try again in a moment, or sign in again and retry.
settings.apiKeyGeneratedHint: This key is generated for the currently signed-in account and is reused by the domains page automatically. No manual input is required.
settings.apiKeyLoading: Syncing the current account management API key...
settings.apiKeyEmptyState: No management API key is available for this account yet.
settings.apiKeyLoginHint: Sign in to an account, then generate its management API key here.
settings.apiKeyDescription: Providing an API Key gives you access to more domain choices and private domain creation permissions. Current Context API Key: {apiKey}
settings.apiKeyFormatWarning: API Key format may be incorrect, should start with 'dk_'
settings.testComplete: API Key test completed, check console logs
settings.domainPageDescription: Manage globally available domains for {provider}, inspect DNS records, and trigger verification from the admin console.
settings.domainManagementDescription: Use the admin console to add domains, inspect DNS records, and trigger verification.
settings.domainInputDescription: Second-level and third-level domains are supported. New domains generate three records: a mail-subdomain CNAME, a domain MX, and a TXT ownership token.
settings.domainInputDescriptionCloudflare: When Cloudflare is configured, you can submit multiple third-level domains at once with one domain per line or comma-separated. TmpMail creates them one by one and auto-syncs DNS when Cloudflare auto-sync is enabled.
settings.domainInputRequired: Enter a domain before creating it
settings.domainBatchDialogDescription: Choose a managed second-level domain as the root, then generate third-level domains in the form prefix-random.root-domain.
settings.domainBatchRootDescription: TmpMail generates domains in the form prefix-random.root-domain.
settings.domainBatchPrefixDescription: Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only.
settings.domainBatchRandomLengthDescription: Recommended: {min} to {max} characters. Longer values reduce collisions.
settings.domainBatchCountDescription: Generate up to {count} domains per batch.
settings.domainManagementNeedsApiKey: Administrator access is required before managed domains can be created or verified.
settings.domainManagementNeedsApiKeyHint: Open the Admin page, save the administrator API key, then return here.
settings.managedDomainsEmptyHint: New domains start pending verification. After activation, admins can decide whether to expose them for public signup.
settings.domainBatchCreatedDescription: Created {created} domains, skipped {skipped} existing domains, and failed {failed}.
settings.domainBatchCreatedWithSyncDescription: Created {created} domains, Cloudflare synced {synced}, pending manual follow-up {pending}, skipped {skipped} existing domains, and failed {failed}.
settings.domainBatchAllSkippedDesc: All submitted domains already exist in the managed domain list.
settings.domainBatchRequiresCloudflareDesc: Enable Cloudflare in Settings and save a working API token before batch-adding third-level domains.
settings.domainBatchNoSecondLevelDomainDesc: The current token did not return any accessible zones. Check the token permissions, account ownership, and zone scope.
settings.cloudflareZoneLoadingDesc: TmpMail is fetching the zones accessible by the currently saved token.
settings.cloudflareZoneLoadTimeoutDesc: Loading the Cloudflare zone list timed out. Please try again.
settings.cloudflareZoneLoadFailedDesc: Unable to read the zones accessible by the current token. Check whether the token is still valid.
settings.cloudflareZoneApiOutdatedDesc: The current backend only returns the zone count, not the full zone list. Redeploy and rebuild the API container, then reopen this dialog.
settings.domainBatchRootRequiredDesc: For example, enter fuckcyh.de and TmpMail will generate third-level domains like app-abc123.fuckcyh.de.
settings.domainBatchRootInvalidDesc: The root domain should look like example.com. TmpMail prepends the prefix and random suffix in front of it.
settings.domainBatchPrefixRequiredDesc: Use a readable prefix such as app, mail, or tmp.
settings.domainBatchPrefixInvalidDesc: The prefix may only contain lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens, and must be at most {count} characters long.
settings.domainBatchPrefixTooLong: Prefix and random suffix are too long together
settings.domainBatchPrefixTooLongDesc: A single subdomain label cannot exceed 63 characters. Shorten the prefix or reduce the random length.
settings.domainBatchThirdLevelOnly: Batch mode only supports third-level domains
settings.domainBatchThirdLevelOnlyDesc: {domain} is not a third-level domain. Use a format like app.example.com and try again.
settings.domainBatchLimitExceededDesc: You can batch-add up to {count} third-level domains at a time.
settings.domainBatchLimitReachedDesc: This account can add {remaining} more domains. Reduce the batch size and try again.
settings.domainBatchGenerateFailedDesc: Too many random collisions occurred. Try again or reduce the batch size.
settings.dnsRecordsReady: DNS records are ready. Complete the records, then run verification.
settings.dnsRecordsPartialLoadFailed: Domain was created, but DNS records could not be loaded yet. Expand the domain later to retry.
settings.dnsRecordsHint: Apply these records in DNS. The worker will keep polling automatically, and you can also verify manually.
settings.domainDeleteConfirm: Delete {domain}? The request will be rejected if mailbox accounts still exist on this domain.
settings.domainDeleteFailedDesc: Make sure no mailbox accounts are still using this domain.
settings.domainVerifyPendingDesc: Wait for DNS propagation and verify again. The worker will also keep checking.
settings.domainVerifyFailedDesc: Check whether the DNS records have propagated correctly.

admin.title: TmpMail
admin.headline: Initialize the first administrator
admin.entryHeroDescription: TmpMail provides a simple temporary mail service for verification codes, sign-up emails, and one-time notifications.
admin.entryPanelDescription: Create an inbox and start receiving mail right away for registration, testing, and other short-term use cases.
admin.loginIdentityRequired: Enter a username or email first
admin.registerOtpHint: Before registering, send a one-time verification code to this email address and enter it here.
admin.registerOtpSentDescription: A verification code was sent to this email address and expires in {seconds} seconds.
admin.registerOtpSendFailed: Failed to send the OTP code
admin.registerOtpSendFailedDescription: Please try again later, or check whether the email address is available.
admin.setupSuccessDescription: The first administrator was created and the current key has been issued.
admin.setupFailedDescription: Unable to save the first administrator. Check the input and try again.
admin.loginFailedDescription: Check the username, email, and password and try again.
admin.registerSuccessDescription: The workspace member account was created. Entering TmpMail now.
admin.registerFailedDescription: Check the email, password, or current registration policy and try again.
admin.registrationClosedDescription: This site does not allow public registration right now. Contact an administrator or use Linux Do instead.
admin.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 10 characters
admin.passwordMismatch: The two passwords do not match
admin.statusLoadFailedDesc: Unable to read the current console configuration state.
admin.secureContextRequiredDescription: Sign-in, password changes, and key issuance must happen over HTTPS or a trusted local address.
admin.secureContextReadyHint: This connection is eligible for sensitive actions.
admin.insecureContextDescription: This connection is not secure, so sign-in, password changes, and key issuance are blocked. Switch to HTTPS or use a trusted local address first.
admin.recoveryDescription: When an administrator forgets the password, use the recovery token to reset the password by username and rotate a new key.
admin.recoveryUnavailableDescription: TMPMAIL_ADMIN_RECOVERY_TOKEN is not configured on the server, so recovery is unavailable right now.
admin.recoverySuccessDescription: The password has been reset and a new key was rotated.
admin.recoveryFailedDescription: Check the recovery token or username and try again.
admin.linuxDoSubmit: Register / Sign in with Linux Do
admin.linuxDoInviteCodeHint: This site requires an invite code, and Linux Do registration will use the same code above.
admin.linuxDoUnavailableDescription: Linux Do sign-in is not configured yet, or public registration is unavailable on this site.
admin.linuxDoClientSecretPlaceholder: Enter the Linux Do OAuth app secret
admin.linuxDoClientSecretPlaceholderConfigured: A secret is already saved. Enter a new one to replace it
admin.linuxDoClientSecretHint: Sensitive data is stored on the server and is never sent back to the browser.
admin.linuxDoClientSecretConfiguredHint: The current secret is already stored on the server. Leave this blank to keep it unchanged.
admin.linuxDoCallbackDescription: Validating the authorization result and creating the current session now.
admin.linuxDoCallbackFailedDescription: Unable to finish the current Linux Do sign-in flow. Return to TmpMail and try again.
admin.linuxDoCodeMissing: Linux Do did not return a valid authorization code.
admin.linuxDoStateMismatch: The Linux Do sign-in state expired. Start the sign-in flow again.
admin.mailboxCreateRequired: Enter a mailbox prefix and choose a domain suffix
admin.mailboxCreateLocalPartInvalid: The mailbox prefix must not include @ or a full domain
admin.mailboxCreatePreviewPlaceholder: The full mailbox address will be previewed here after you choose a domain
admin.mailboxCreateSecretHint: A separate mailbox password is no longer required here. The workspace issues the access token for you automatically.
admin.mailboxCreateNoDomainAvailable: There is no active managed domain yet. Add and verify a domain first.
admin.mailboxCreateAutoOpenFailedDescription: Open it manually from the mailbox list on the left, or try again in a moment.
admin.mailboxCreateFailedDescription: Check the address format or domain ownership and try again.
admin.mailboxLoadFailedDescription: Unable to read the mailboxes owned by the current console user.
admin.mailboxAccessFailedDescription: Unable to issue a mailbox access token for this address.
admin.mailboxDeleteConfirm: Delete mailbox {address}? This also removes the mailbox messages.
admin.mailboxDeleteFailedDescription: Please try again in a moment.
admin.mailboxEmptyDescription: Create your first mailbox on a domain you manage, then open it here.
admin.mailboxMessagesEmptyDescription: Select a mailbox on the left to load its messages and history.
admin.systemEnabledDescription: When disabled, regular users can no longer add domains or create mailbox accounts.
admin.openRegistrationDescription: When disabled, public domains are hidden and the frontend can no longer create new mailboxes.
admin.publicDomainsDescription: Selected verified domains will appear in the public domain selector for mailbox signup. Leave everything unselected to keep all active domains available.
admin.publicDomainsEmptyDescription: Add and verify a managed domain first, then come back here to expose it publicly.
admin.allowedEmailSuffixesDescription: Advanced rule: add suffix patterns here. For example, example.com allows all of its subdomains.
admin.emailOtpDescription: Keep this off by default. Only enable it after outbound mail is working reliably; blank subject and body fields will use the default template automatically.
admin.emailOtpAutoTemplateHint: If the subject or body is left blank, the system will fill in the default OTP template automatically.
admin.emailOtpDefaultBody: Your TmpMail verification code is {code}. It expires in {ttlSeconds} seconds.
admin.smtpSettingsDescription: Configure outbound SMTP for email OTP. Saved settings take effect immediately for future verification emails.
admin.smtpSettingsHint: At minimum, provide the SMTP host and sender address. Fill in the username and password when your SMTP provider requires authentication.
admin.smtpPasswordPlaceholderConfigured: A SMTP password is already saved. Enter a new one to replace it
admin.smtpPasswordHint: Fill in the username and password if your SMTP provider requires authentication.
admin.smtpPasswordConfiguredHint: The SMTP password is already stored on the server. Leave this blank to keep it unchanged.
admin.smtpSecurityPlainHint: Fits plain SMTP ports such as 25 or 2525 and will not upgrade the connection automatically.
admin.smtpSecurityStarttlsHint: Fits submission ports such as 587 and upgrades the connection with STARTTLS.
admin.smtpSecurityTlsHint: Fits implicit TLS ports such as 465 and enables TLS immediately when connecting.
admin.smtpStarttlsOn: STARTTLS is enabled, which fits negotiated TLS ports such as 587.
admin.smtpStarttlsOff: STARTTLS is disabled and the server will connect with plain SMTP.
admin.linuxDoDescription: Configure Linux Do Connect parameters for registration and login verification.
admin.linuxDoQuickstartDescription: Open Linux Do Connect to create an app, then paste the Client ID and Client Secret back here. The fixed OAuth endpoints and this site's callback URL are listed below.
admin.linuxDoReferenceDescription: Use the authorize, token, and userinfo URLs as shown below. For the redirect URI, use the callback URL shown here.
admin.linuxDoClientIdHint: Enter the client ID from your Linux Do OAuth app.
admin.linuxDoCallbackUrlHint: The callback URL must exactly match the Redirect URI configured in your Linux Do app. Update it there too if this site's domain changes.
admin.linuxDoMinimumTrustLevelHint: The minimum trust level allowed to register.
admin.defaultDomainLimitLabel: Default domain limit for new members
admin.cloudflarePanelDescription: Configure a Cloudflare API token for the current account so domain DNS records can be written in one click.
admin.cloudflareDescription: Both regular members and administrators can save their own Cloudflare token here and use it to write DNS records for domains they own.
admin.cloudflareTokenPlaceholder: Enter a token with Zone:Read and DNS:Edit permissions
admin.cloudflareTokenPlaceholderConfigured: A token is already saved. Enter a new one to replace it
admin.cloudflareTokenConfiguredHint: A token is already stored on the server. Leave this blank to keep it unchanged.
admin.cloudflareAutoSyncOn: Auto-sync DNS when a domain is created
admin.cloudflareAutoSyncOff: Do not auto-sync DNS when a domain is created
admin.cloudflareAutoSyncDescription: When enabled, new managed domains will push their verification DNS records to Cloudflare automatically.
admin.cloudflareQuickHelpDescription: After saving, you can click the cloud button on each domain card for a manual sync or let new domains sync automatically.
admin.cloudflarePermissionsHint: The token must cover the target zone and include Zone:Read plus DNS:Edit.
admin.cloudflareLoadFailedDescription: Unable to read the Cloudflare settings for the current account right now.
admin.cloudflareTokenRequired: Provide a valid API token before enabling Cloudflare automation.
admin.cloudflareSaveFailedDescription: Check the token permissions and try again.
admin.cloudflareTestNoZones: Token is valid, but has no zones
admin.cloudflareTestNoZonesDescription: Validation passed, but the token cannot access any zones.
admin.cloudflareTestFailedDescription: The token is invalid or cannot access the Cloudflare API.
admin.cloudflareSyncFailedDescription: Unable to sync the DNS records to Cloudflare right now. Check the token, zone permissions, or domain ownership.
admin.cloudflareSyncDeferredDescription: The domain was added, but there is no usable Cloudflare sync configuration yet. Finish the setup in Settings and run a manual sync later.
admin.dnsDesignDescription: Use the same three-record pattern for every managed domain: a stable mail host, a domain MX, and a verification TXT.
admin.mailExchangeHostHint: For example, mail.example.com. Every managed domain MX points here.
admin.mailRouteTargetHint: Enter the final target behind the shared host, such as a fixed IP or load balancer entry.
admin.domainTxtPrefixHint: For example, _tmpmail-verify. The system will build the full TXT host automatically.
admin.dnsFlowDescription: Set up the shared mail host first, then let users bind domains one by one. When you move servers, you usually only update the shared host target.
admin.secureContextDescription: Always access the public console over HTTPS so sessions, passwords, and management keys are not exposed on a plaintext path.
admin.settingsSaveFailedDescription: Check the input and try again.
admin.settingsUnsavedChangesConfirm: You have unsaved settings changes. Leave this section and discard them?

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: TmpMail Privacy Policy
privacy.description: This document explains how TmpMail collects, uses, and protects limited personal information that may be processed while you use this site.
privacy.dataCollectionContent: As on many websites, the site editor may automatically receive general information that is contained in server log files, such as your IP address, and cookie information. Information about how advertising may be served on this site (if it is indeed the site editor's policy to display advertising) is set forth below.
privacy.dataUseContent: Data may be used to customize and improve your user experience on this site. Efforts will be made to prevent your data from being made available to third parties unless (i) provided for otherwise in this Privacy Policy; (ii) your consent is obtained, such as when you choose to opt-in or opt-out for the sharing of data; (iii) a service provided on our site requires interaction with a third party, or is provided by a third party, such as an application service provider; (iv) pursuant to legal action or law enforcement; (v) it is found that your use of this site violates the site editor's policy, terms of service, or other usage guidelines, or if it is deemed reasonably necessary by the site editor to protect the site editor's legal rights and/or property; (vi) this site is purchased by a third party, in which case that third party will be able to use the data in the same manner as set forth in this policy.
privacy.cookiesContent: Like many websites, this website sets and uses cookies to enhance your user experience — to remember your personal settings, for instance. Advertisements may display on this website and, if so, may set and access cookies on your computer; such cookies are subject to the privacy policy of the parties providing the advertisement. However, the parties providing the advertising do not have access to this site's cookies. These parties usually use non-personally-identifiable or anonymous codes to obtain information about your visits to this site.
privacy.tempEmailSecurityContent: Traditional inboxes often keep data for long periods. TmpMail is designed to minimize retained mailbox data, protect account access with credentials stored in your browser, and reduce unnecessary exposure while you handle temporary verification workflows.
privacy.dataDeletionContent: All emails and data temporarily stored in our service will be permanently deleted. You can delete your temporary email address at any time using the corresponding Delete button on the homepage. Your privacy is our top priority. You don't need to worry about your data. We will provide comprehensive protection. Access to your data will only be provided to you personally, and only during the validity period of the temporary email address.
privacy.userCareContent: We care about all our users. The management of this website can make changes to this privacy policy.

faqSection.useCasesTitle: Common Use Cases for Temporary Email
faqSection.useCase1: Register on websites or apps without exposing your real email address — avoid spam and promotional emails forever after.
faqSection.useCase2: Sign up for forums, trial platforms, or software testing with no privacy concerns or long-term commitments.
faqSection.useCase3: Developers can test email-sending features without cluttering real inboxes — fast and disposable.
faqSection.useCase4: When a website requires email verification but you prefer not to share your real address, a temporary email is the perfect solution.
faqSection.step1Desc: Open TmpMail and create a temporary mailbox in seconds with no registration and no waiting.
faqSection.step2Desc: Copy the generated address and paste it into any signup form that requires email verification.
faqSection.step3Desc: Check the TmpMail inbox and your verification code or confirmation email should arrive within seconds.
faqSection.q1: What is a temporary email address?
faqSection.a1: A temporary email address (also known as a disposable or throwaway email) is a short-term inbox that can receive messages without requiring personal registration details. TmpMail provides free, instant temporary mailboxes to help protect your privacy online.
faqSection.q2: Is TmpMail completely free to use?
faqSection.a2: Yes. TmpMail can be used without account registration fees, software downloads, or a separate signup flow. Open the site and create a mailbox when you need one.
faqSection.q3: How long does my temporary email address last?
faqSection.a3: Your temporary email remains active as long as you don't delete the account. Your login credentials are saved in your browser's local storage, so you can return later and continue using the same address. If you clear your browser data, you can recreate the account with the same username and continue receiving emails.
faqSection.q4: Is using a temporary email safe?
faqSection.a4: TmpMail protects your inbox with a password stored in your browser session. Using a temporary mailbox for third-party registrations helps shield your real email from spam, data breaches, and unwanted contact.
faqSection.q5: Can I send emails with TmpMail?
faqSection.a5: TmpMail is currently focused on receive-only workflows. Use your regular email provider when you need outbound mail.
faqSection.q6: Who should use a temporary email service?
faqSection.a6: Anyone who values online privacy can benefit from a disposable email. It's especially useful for everyday users who want to avoid spam, developers testing email workflows, people joining events or trials without sharing real contact details, and anyone needing a quick one-time email verification.
faqSection.q7: Is my email content stored or read by anyone?
faqSection.a7: TmpMail runs on its own self-hosted backend API. Messages are stored temporarily and should be treated as short-lived test data, so save anything important promptly. Only the mailbox credentials stored in your browser can be used to access the inbox.

apiDocs.title: API Documentation
apiDocs.subtitle: Welcome to the TmpMail API
apiDocs.description: This API allows you to automate the registration process at various sites which require email confirmation for testing purposes. Usage of our API for illegal activity is strictly prohibited.
apiDocs.authDescription: Most endpoints require authentication via a Bearer Token. You can obtain a token by providing an address and password.
apiDocs.apiKeyDescription: Domain-management endpoints require the administrator API key generated in the admin console after the admin password is configured. The plaintext key is only shown when issued or rotated, and public deployments should use HTTPS before transmitting it. Regular mailbox accounts do not manage domains.
apiDocs.contributionsDescription: We welcome developers to create integrations, libraries, and tools for the TmpMail API. If you have contributions, feel free to contact us or submit a pull request on the GitHub repository.
apiDocs.llmDocsDescription: Plain-text API reference. Share the link with AI assistants to help write integration code.
apiDocs.responsePlaceholder: // Run the request to preview the response here.
apiDocs.domainGetDesc: Get the list of active public domains. If an administrator API key is provided, pending and inactive managed domains are also returned.
apiDocs.domainCreateDesc: Create a managed domain from the admin console. Requires the administrator API key and returns the new domain in pending verification state.
apiDocs.domainRecordsDesc: Get the CNAME, MX, and TXT records required to activate a managed domain.
apiDocs.domainVerifyDesc: Run an immediate DNS verification for a managed domain and return its latest status.
apiDocs.accountCreateDesc: Create a new email account. Optional expiresIn parameter sets account lifetime in seconds (0 or -1 = never expires, omit = 24h default). Any active domain can be used directly.
apiDocs.accountMeDesc: Get information about the currently authenticated account.
apiDocs.accountDeleteDesc: Delete the currently authenticated email account by ID. You can only delete your own account.
apiDocs.tokenDesc: Get an authentication token using an email address and password.
apiDocs.messageListDesc: Get the list of messages in the inbox.
apiDocs.messageGetDesc: Get detailed information for a single message by its ID.
apiDocs.messageDeleteDesc: Delete a message by its ID.
